skillName: Substituting into expressions with one operation
misconceptionSummary: Students replace the variable incorrectly or forget that adjacency means multiplication.

steps.narration: To substitute, replace the variable with its value.
Since a equals four, replace a with four. Three a becomes three times four.
Now calculate. Three times four equals twelve.
The same method works for other one-step expressions. Replace the variable, then work out the answer.

visuals.label: Replace the variable with its value

steps.narration: The method is always the same. Put in the value of the variable, then evaluate the expression.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: reading 3a as 34 when a = 4. 3a means 3 multiplied by a, so it becomes 3 × 4, not 34.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: When a variable sits next to a number, it means multiplication. Three a with a equals four becomes three times four, not thirty-four.
